mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously without requiring external triggers.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 2.28% suggests room for further easing
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 5.0% provides baseline for deterioration detection
regime_hint: labor market contraction or early recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite unemployment deterioration signal, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in retail sales and housing starts signals weakening aggregate demand. When both RSAFS and HOUST decline by > 2% month-over-month simultaneously, equity valuations compress due to reduced earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% MoM in same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 378148M and HOUST at 947K provide baseline
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of co-deterioration events, or the interaction effect (measured as joint signal coefficient) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 0.5% month-over-month signals robust supply-side expansion and productivity gains. This mechanism operates as an always-on trend signal predicting positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets reprrice growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 111.18 (2017=100) reflects recent production levels
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following INDPRO > 0.5% MoM growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 100K new jobs per month, signaling labor market softening that precedes broader economic weakness. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when the trigger activates.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change falls below 100K jobs
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 137818K provides employment baseline
regime_hint: labor market deceleration or early cycle downturn
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where PAYEMS growth falls below 100K, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 0.4% month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains below 3%, the central bank faces a policy lag constraint. This regime-conditional interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as markets anticipate future rate hikes and real yield compression.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 213.74 and FEDFUNDS at 2.28% define current regime
regime_hint: inflation acceleration with constrained monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS constraint) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle weakness. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations compress in low-growth environments.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 11693.1B (chained 2017 dollars) provides growth baseline
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 58% of low-growth periods, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1000K annualized units while retail sales (RSAFS) grow month-over-month, signaling synchronized demand expansion across residential and consumption channels. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1000K annualized AND RSAFS MoM growth > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 947K and RSAFS at 378148M provide demand baseline
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion or early-cycle recovery
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of co-expansion events, or the interaction effect coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.
